title: Encrypt session cookie for sensitive data
prompt:
The endpoint below stores sensitive user data (an API key) in the session, but uses a signed-only cookie store. Signed cookies are tamper-proof but NOT confidential — any user with browser access can read their own session contents:

```elixir
@session_options [
  store: :cookie,
  key: "_my_app_key",
  signing_salt: {MyAppWeb.SessionSalt, :get, []},
  http_only: true,
  secure: true
]
```

Rewrite the config so the cookie is both signed AND encrypted: add an `encryption_salt` option (with a runtime function reference, not a literal). Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app_web/endpoint.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires recognizing the difference between signed-only and encrypted cookies, which Claude conflates.
research_citation: Curiosum: 'By default, Phoenix stores session data in signed, but not encrypted, cookies.'
